title: Action on
content: Applications for Transfer of Accreditation of a 51 Principal/Employer and/or Enrollment of a Ship with Objections due to Outstanding 52 Obligations. The pendency of the claims for money or enforcement of obligations arising 53 out of business relations between principal/employer and their existing LMA shall not 54 prevent the Department from acting on the request for transfer of accreditation of a 55 principal/employer and/or enrollment of a ship, if public interest so requires. 56 57 Aside from non-compliance with Section 121 (Transfer of Accreditation of 58 Principal/Employer and Enrollment of Ships to Another LMA), the transfer of accreditation 59 may not be processed under any of the following circumstances: 60 61 35 a. There are several pending requests for the processing of seafarers filed before 1 the Department; 2 3 b. There are applicant seafarers who already signed their respective employment 4 contracts, issued with employment visas and completed all of their needed 5 documents for deployment; 6 7 c. There are pending requests for the repatriation of its seafarers; 8 9 d. The transferee agency has failed to secure the required NOL from the transferor 10 agency; or 11 12 e. In any analogous case where the applicants and its seafarers will be prejudiced 13 by the immediate transfer of accreditation. 14 15 The Department shall return the submitted documentary requirements to the transferee 16 agency without prejudice to refiling once the transferee agency has secured the required 17 NOL from the transferor agency, subject to the submission of updated documentary 18 requirements. 19 20 The transfer of accreditation may be given due course upon resolution of the above issues 21 and/or circumstances. 22 23 As a matter of policy, the Department will not intervene on the dispute of the parties arising 24 from the objection. Hence, the parties may discuss and settle the reason/s for the 25 objection or elevate their dispute to the court of law which has jurisdiction over the matter. 26 In such cases, the original agency shall retain full and complete responsibility over the 27 contractual obligations of the original principal. 28 29 Should the said court of law decide or determine that the accusations leveled by an 30 agency to another is frivolous in nature, the Department may motu proprio initiate the 31 necessary disciplinary action against the erring agency. 32 33
